subcodes.title: Words spoken at Pleiades Sylvan Bower, Alhammeh near Tiberias, 1914-05-10
subcodes.first_line: Vanity is a form of egotism, an excessive desire for notice of approval of one's personal appearance or deeds. It is self-conceit and self-hypnotization.
subcodes.subjects: @humility; meekness; lowliness, @pride; vanity; laying claim to inner knowledge, @Satan; the Evil One
